title: FIX-467: Bug Creating First Library Ticket — Root Cause & Fix Walkthrough
description: This tour walks through the complete lifecycle of FIX-467, a bug where creating the first library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours of waiting. The root cause was that the system was incorrectly using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories — specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repos. Multiple agent runs were required to surface and fully resolve this issue. The tour follows the investigation in data-flow order: from initial user report, through agent triage runs, to the final corrected token-selection logic and verification.

steps.description: Entry point: the original ticket FIX-467 filed by the user. The user reported that creating a new library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours of waiting. Screenshots were attached as evidence of the failure. A critical constraint is noted here — the agent's available database is not an exact copy of production, but the fix must ensure the new library is created regardless. This ticket context file seeds the entire investigation and sets the scope: the problem is environment-specific (staging) and involves the artifact creation pipeline.
steps.title: Step 1: Original Ticket Report (FIX-467)

steps.description: In this second run, the Helix agent acknowledged the ticket and began triage. The discussion section records the first agent response at 2026-05-15T00:13:39Z. No code changes were landed yet — this run focused on understanding the failure mode from attached screenshots and available database state. Data flow at this stage: user report → agent acknowledgment → environment inspection. The agent is scoping the problem before proposing a fix.
steps.title: Step 2: Agent Acknowledges and Begins Triage

steps.description: This run represents the first attempted fix. The agent reported changes ready and provided verification screenshots. However, the fix addressed only a surface symptom — it did not correct the underlying token-selection logic. The user (Usher) followed up at 2026-05-15T03:30:52Z clarifying that the system should not use the user's token for NetSuite file cabinet repo creation; it must use the built-in Helix system token. This discussion entry is the pivotal moment where the actual root cause is identified.
steps.title: Step 3: First Fix Attempt — Surface Symptom Addressed, Root Cause Missed

steps.description: This run captures the agent's deeper investigation following the user's clarification about the token issue. The agent confirmed understanding of the root cause: when creating repositories (especially NetSuite file cabinet repos), the system was incorrectly forwarding the user's OAuth token instead of using the Helix system token reserved for repository provisioning operations. The fix in this run targeted the token-selection logic directly. Data flow corrected: repo creation request → system checks repo type → selects Helix system token (not user token) → creates repository successfully.
steps.title: Step 4: Root Cause Confirmed — Token Selection Logic Fix

steps.description: This CodeTour file was generated during the investigation run to help developers navigate the changes. It documents the multi-run structure and the evolving understanding of the bug. Note that this walkthrough was superseded by later, more complete versions as the investigation progressed.
steps.title: Step 5: Intermediate CodeTour — Investigation Navigation Aid

steps.description: An updated CodeTour generated in a subsequent run, refining the walkthrough with more accurate data-flow descriptions. This version more precisely documents the token-selection correction and the NetSuite file cabinet repo-specific code path that was affected. It serves as an intermediate navigation artifact for reviewers examining the multi-run fix history.
steps.title: Step 6: Updated CodeTour — Refined Token-Selection Documentation

steps.description: The final run ticket context, representing the fully resolved state of FIX-467. The complete discussion history is preserved here, showing the progression from initial report through multiple triage and fix iterations to the confirmed resolution. This file is the authoritative record of the fix lifecycle and includes the agent's final explanation of how the token-selection logic was corrected to always use the Helix system token for repository creation operations, regardless of the calling user's context.
steps.title: Step 7: Final Run — Fully Resolved Ticket State

steps.description: The definitive CodeTour for this fix, generated in the final resolution run. It captures the complete data-flow story: user report → agent triage (multiple runs) → root cause identification (wrong token used for NetSuite repo creation) → token-selection logic fix → verification. All major code paths remain clean: the fix is scoped to the repository provisioning layer and does not affect user-facing authentication flows or other token usage contexts. Reviewers can use this tour as the canonical reference for understanding FIX-467.
steps.title: Step 8: Final CodeTour — Canonical Fix Reference & Code Path Verification
